commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Maintainer/security review and contributor-supplied live proof are needed; the remaining blockers are not a safe autonomous repair-only task.; Needs attention: The diff introduces a concrete reasoning-privacy concern by publishing raw Claude thinking before the established visibility gate.; Review finding: [P1] Gate CLI thinking before shared event broadcast — src/agents/cli-runner/execute.ts:1029-1032 - [P2]... (sha=363a9b499dee103eeaf57cfa24ad639209044ea5)
commands.review_summary:
The branch parses Claude CLI `thinking_delta` records into reasoning events, switches the CLI reasoning bridge to `stream: "thinking"`, changes progress/Telegram draft rendering, forwards CLI tool IDs, and adds focused tests.

PR surface: Source +149, Tests +522. Total +671 across 12 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main drops Claude CLI `thinking_delta` records while the existing `/reasoning stream` and progress preview paths can consume reasoning streams. I did not run a live Claude CLI channel reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainer/security review and contributor-supplied live proof are needed; the remaining blockers are not a safe autonomous repair-only task.

target.title: fix(tls): add subjectAltName to auto-generated gateway self-signed certificate (fixes #96910)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #96910.

When gateway TLS is enabled with `autoGenerate=true`, the self-signed certificate had no `subjectAltName` extension. Node.js ≥17 ignores the CN for hostname/IP verification per RFC 6125, causing CLI probe to `wss://127.0.0.1:18789` to fail.

## Changes Made

- New certs: Added `-addext subjectAltName=DNS:localhost,DNS:<hostname>,IP:127.0.0.1,IP:::1` to the openssl command
- Existing certs: Auto-detect CN-only certs (`CN=openclaw-gateway`, no SAN) and regenerate with SANs
- Transactional upgrade: old files renamed to `.bak`, restored on failure
- Custom certs (non-OpenClaw CN) are never touched

## Evidence

- **Unit tests:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/tls/gateway.test.ts` — 10/10 passed

## Real behavior proof

```
# New cert: SAN verified via openssl
$ openssl x509 -in gateway-cert.pem -noout -ext subjectAltName
X509v3 Subject Alternative Name:
    DNS:localhost, DNS:LIN-66D8BD4EA2C, IP Address:127.0.0.1, IP Address:0:0:0:0:0:0:0:1

# Strict TLS check passes
$ node -e "tls.checkServerIdentity('127.0.0.1', cert)"
✅ STRICT TLS CHECK PASSED for 127.0.0.1

# Upgrade path: CN-only cert auto-detected and regenerated
[warn] gateway tls: upgrading CN-only certificate to include subjectAltName
[info] gateway tls: generated self-signed cert at ...

# Custom cert not touched
BEFORE: CN=custom-server, no SAN
AFTER:  CN=custom-server, no SAN (unchanged)
```

- ✅ New certs include subjectAltName
- ✅ Strict TLS identity check passes for IP and hostname
- ✅ Existing CN-only certs auto-upgraded with transactional backup/rollback
- ✅ Custom certificates (non-OpenClaw) are never rewritten

- [x] AI-assisted
target.author: zw-xysk
